job_statement: When I need to hire a first designer or a freelancer for my early-stage product, I want to see evidence that this person can work independently from research through to shipped UI, so I can feel confident they won't need constant direction from me.
context: A founder has decided they need design help but has no design team to interview alongside them. They are evaluating whether to hire full-time, contract, or freelance. They are looking for proof of autonomy — can this person own the design process without being managed? The portfolio is usually discovered via a warm intro or LinkedIn and reviewed before a first call.
current_solutions: Warm intros from other founders or designers
Agencies as a fallback when a solo hire seems too risky
Take-home project or paid trial to de-risk
desired_outcome: After reviewing the portfolio, the founder is confident enough to schedule a call. The portfolio proves: this person has worked in resource-constrained environments, owned the full design process, and shipped something real.
portfolio_implications: 0→1 work is more valuable than big-company contribution — Aysa (2-week MVP) and Keytrn (sole designer) are the lead stories for this audience
Breadth matters more than depth — showing research + systems + UI tells the founder they won't need to hire three people
The fact that Rob built this site himself (Astro, React, Framer Motion) is a differentiator — make it visible
Copy should be direct and first-person — founders respond to a clear point of view, not polished corporate language
Outcomes need business context: 15k views, $2.1M pitch deck, acquisition — not just design metrics
Implementation evidence (built it yourself, shipped to production) is a major differentiator for founder audiences
Showing you came back to fix something after shipping it signals maturity and ownership
Name collaborators by role to prove cross-functional capability without overclaiming
